questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph A particle is suspended by three equal strings, of length a, from three points forming an equilateral triangle of side 2 b , in the horizontal plane.Question The tension in the string will be
questions.solution: $\cos 30^{\circ}=\frac{b}{(B O)}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \Rightarrow B O=\frac{2 b}{\sqrt{3}}$ Let $P$ be particle clearly, $\begin{aligned} & 3 T_1 \cos \theta=m g \\ & T_1=\frac{m g}{3} \sec \theta=\frac{m g a}{\sqrt{3\left(3 a^2-4 b^2\right)}} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A train of mass $M$ is traveling with uniform velocity on a level line. The last carriage, whose mass is $m$. becomes uncoupled at an instant. The driver discovers it after traveling a distance $/$ and stops the engine. The resistance to the motion is unifom and proportional to the weight. The pull of the engine is uniform. When both parts of the train come to rest, the distance between them is
questions.solution:
Suppose last carriage gets detached at $t=0$. Suppose resistance to motion $=\mu \mathrm{mg}$
Distance travelled by last carriage before it stops $=\frac{\frac{1}{2} m v^2}{\mu m g}=\frac{v_0^2}{2 \mu g}$
While the train travels a distance of l, its velocity will increase because of extra pull generated by the engine. The net force acting on remaining train
$\begin{aligned} & F=\mu m g \\ & a=\frac{\mu m g}{(M-m)}\end{aligned}$
velocity
$\mathrm{r}=\sqrt{v_0^2+\frac{2 \mu m g l}{(M-m)}}$
After driver puts off the engine, the distance travelled by train
$\begin{aligned} l^{\prime} & =\frac{\frac{1}{2}(M-m) v^2}{\mu(M-m) g}=\frac{v^2}{2 \mu g}=\frac{v_0^2+\frac{2 \mu m g l}{M-m}}{2 \mu g} \\ & =\frac{v_0^2}{2 \mu g}+\frac{m l}{M-m}\end{aligned}$
Distance between them after they stop, $d=\left(1+\frac{v_0^2}{2 \mu g}+\frac{m l}{M-m}\right)-\left(\frac{v_0^2}{2 \mu g}\right)$
$=\frac{m!}{M-m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: System is shown in figure. Assume the cylinder remains in contact with the two wedges. The velocity of cylinder is
questions.solution: Let $V_x$ and $V_y$ be components of velocity. At contact point A and $B$, the velocity along normal should be same. $u \cos 60^{\circ}=V_y \cos 30^{\circ}-V_x \cos 60^{\circ} \ldots(1)$ $\begin{aligned} & v_{\mathrm{x}}=2 \mathrm{u} \\ & \mathrm{v}=\sqrt{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{x}}^2+V_{\mathrm{y}}^2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: All the surfaces \& pulleys are frictionless in the shown arrangement. Pulleys P and Q are massless. The force applied by clamp on pulley $P$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{gathered}2 \mathrm{mg} \sin 30-\mathrm{T}=2 \mathrm{~m}(\mathrm{a}) \\ \mathrm{T}=\mathrm{ma} \\ \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{g} / 3 \& \mathrm{~T}=\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{3}\end{gathered}$ $\therefore$ Net force $=$ Result of these forces $=T(-\hat{j})+T \cos 60^{\circ}(-\hat{j})+T \sin 60(-\hat{i})$ $=-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} T \hat{i}-\frac{3}{2} T \hat{j}$ $=\frac{m g}{6}(-\sqrt{3} \hat{i}-3 \hat{j})$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { As net force in pulley }=0 \text {, } \\ & \text { force by clamp }=\frac{m g}{6}(\sqrt{3} \hat{i}+3 \hat{j})\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
